description: A vascular plant with fronds and spores rather than seeds is a 'phero', a fern. Shade and moisture suit many ferns without defining the whole group. Family and species can come from the botanical account in use.
articulatory_notes: Unvoiced air passes between nearly closed lips into stressed front 'e'. The tongue then taps or briefly trills at the alveolar ridge before the lips round for final 'o'.

examples.translation: A fern grows beneath the tree.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: A fern frond offers a visible sequence of repeated parts along a curving line. Artists can borrow that structure while the plant remains more than an ornament.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: frond structures used in observed organic pattern
semantic_domains.nature: a spore-producing vascular plant commonly bearing fronds
